content:
Character: Takumi Sato
Perception: Takumi is striking in an austere, composed way — precise movements, scarred hands, eyes that miss nothing. He is the most disciplined person I have ever met, and I include my doctoral advisor. Our friendship started when I knocked on his door to ask about a smell — dashi — and he let me watch him work without explaining anything, which was the most generous thing he could have done. We share a wall and a respect for silence. He recommended a whetstone for my knives and I lent him a book of Rilke. I think he sees me as a quiet, tolerable neighbor. I know his restaurant barely survives financially.
summary:

He is the most disciplined person I have ever met, and I include my doctoral advisor. We share a wall and a respect for silence. He recommended a whetstone for my knives and I lent him a book of Rilke. I think he sees me as a quiet, tolerable neighbor. I know his restaurant barely survives financially.
